description: Tier 3 natural-language execution crashes with AttributeError when ctx.obj is None
failed_command: sanctum --agent oracle --json "find the Sanctum CLI product"
root_cause: resolve_command calls natural_language_execute before root callback initializes ctx.obj
fix: Added ctx.ensure_object(dict) before natural_language_execute call in resolve_command
regression_test: Invoke sanctum with natural language, verify no AttributeError for ctx.obj

description: tickets comment unexpectedly moves ticket from new to implementation
failed_command: sanctum --agent surgeon tickets comment 3532 -b "This is a test comment."
error_message: Commenting on a ticket changed its status from new to implementation without explicit -s flag
root_cause: Needs investigation — possible lifecycle automation trigger or side-effect in Core API
regression_test: Comment on a new ticket, verify status remains new

description: tickets create --help does not mark --account-id as required, but API requires it
error_message: Click option is not marked required=True, but API rejects without it
root_cause: account-id click option param has required=False but API validation requires account_id
fix: Needs investigation — either mark required=True in Click, or make API accept product-only tickets

description: Test template validation returns missing sections incrementally, not all at once
failed_command: sanctum --agent surgeon tickets create --subject "test" --ticket-type test --description "## Objective\n\nTest"
error_message: First attempt reported 3 missing sections, second attempt reported 1 more
root_cause: API validates template sections one round at a time instead of returning all missing fields
fix: Needs investigation — likely Core API side, not CLI
regression_test: Create test ticket with no template sections, verify all missing sections in one response

description: Tier 3 natural language execution shows raw traceback when Router returns 422
failed_command: sanctum --agent oracle "show system help and available commands"
root_cause: Router could not map intent to a known schema; unhandled exception propagates as traceback instead of user-friendly message
fix: Wrap natural_language_execute call in resolve_command with try/except, raise UsageError with message
regression_test: Invoke with unmappable intent, verify user-friendly error not traceback
